articles.title: VENTURES, RILLET, AND CLARIFY
articles.summary: Most first-time founders don't know if they're ready to hire, who the right person is or how to close them. Without guidance, they burn months on bad fits or miss great candidates.

articles.title: EVERYONE IS EXCITED ABOUT CLAUDE
articles.summary: We are watching the democratization of competence in real time. The excitement around tools like Claude Code is about the sudden acceleration of human potential. We are crossing a threshold where software no longer just assists but actively up-levels the user, allowing a mid-level engineer to perform like a senior architect. This signals a massive explosion in software creation from people who previously lacked the technical skill to build what they could imagine.

articles.title: HOW AI MADE PRICING HARD AGAIN
articles.summary: The per-seat model is dying, and nobody knows what will replace it. For twenty years, SaaS pricing was easy: count the humans and multiply by thirty dollars. AI breaks this because its entire purpose is to reduce the number of humans needed to do the work. We are returning to an era of complex, value-based pricing.

articles.title: YOUR #1 JOB FOR JANUARY? DEPLOY AN AI AGENT — YOURSELF. HANDS ON KEYBOARD
articles.summary: Sales, marketing, and GTM leaders need to learn how to deploy their own AI agents. The leaders who will win in the AI era are those who have done the work themselves. While AI jargon may be intimidating, the actual work isn't that hard. This post provides some tips on how to deploy a fully custom AI agent.

articles.title: THE AI PRODUCT DESIGN PLAYBOOK
articles.summary: Your model is not your moat. In a world where intelligence is a commodity, the only defensible advantages are proprietary data and deep workflow integration. AI-first startups often fail because they obsess over the tech rather than the user pain. The winning design pattern is not a chatbot - it is a system that absorbs a messy, complex human workflow and makes it invisible. If your product is just a text box, you are just a wrapper.

articles.title: HOW TO BUILD A WAITLIST
articles.summary: A waitlist is not a parking lot - it is a validation engine. Most founders treat waitlists as a collection of email addresses, but they are actually a tool for testing promises. You do not need a product to build one - you just need a clear, specific promise of an outcome. The goal is not to get ten thousand signups - it is to start twenty real conversations with people who feel the pain so acutely they are willing to wait for the cure.

articles.title: DRIVE BRAND AWARENESS WITH TECH DECISION MAKERS AT SCALE
articles.summary: TLDR puts you in front of your target audience with almost no competition. Each newsletter features only three advertisers max, giving your message the space it deserves. Millions of developers, product managers, CEOs, and other tech decision makers actually see what you have to say. Learn more [10] about running a test campaign.

articles.title: STANDARD CAPITAL WINTER 2026 FUNDING CYCLE (RESOURCE)
articles.summary: Applications are now open for Standard Capital's Winter 2026 funding cycle. The deadline to apply is 9 PM PST.

articles.title: INSTRUCT 2.5
articles.summary: Instruct 2.5 is an autonomous AI agent that executes tasks across connected apps in real time and lets users save successful runs as reusable workflows.

articles.title: 2-B.AI
articles.summary: 2-b.ai is a browser-based task manager that combines a to-do list with AI assistance for capturing, planning, and completing tasks.

articles.title: MERCOR'S ASCENDANCY IN AI SPARKS DISCUSSION ON THE FUTURE OF WORK
articles.summary: Mercor has rapidly emerged as a key player in AI. It recently achieved a $10 billion valuation and raised $350 million in Series C funding. The startup aims to provide high-skilled contractors tailored for AI labs and address AI development's increasingly sophisticated needs. This shift highlights evolving workforce demands and raises questions about future job competitiveness in an AI-driven economy.

articles.title: THE CASE FOR ABUNDANCE
articles.summary: Doomerism is a self-fulfilling prophecy. The current narrative around AI safety often acts as a filter, scaring away the exact thoughtful, conscientious builders we need while leaving the field open to the reckless. Abundance - of code, intelligence, and art - should be the default state of the future. You do not get a vote on how that future is shaped by refusing to participate in the present. Optimism is a moral imperative to ensure the good version gets built.

articles.title: THE STARTUPS ARE THRIVING
articles.summary: Recent startups are achieving unprecedented growth rates, significantly outpacing traditional T2D3 metrics to attract investor interest. Companies like Serval, Runware, Rillet, Yoodli, and FAL reported exponential increases in ARR, showcasing robust traction in the AI sector. This rapid growth underscores a shift in how startups secure capital, setting a new baseline for performance amid escalating competition.

articles.title: BEN HOROWITZ ON FIRING THE EXEC TEAM
articles.summary: If you have to ask the question, you already know the answer.

articles.title: THE JOY OF DELEGATING TO COMPETENCE
articles.summary: The best management hack is hiring people who don't need managing.

articles.title: FOUNDERS SHOULD TRUST VIBES IN HIRING
articles.summary: Early-stage founders should explicitly value interpersonal chemistry alongside skills and interviews, since startups require deep trust and fast conflict resolution.
